title: Special Substring

content.problemStatement:
Chef gave you a string SSS of size NNN which contains only lowercase English letters  and asked you to find the length of the longest substring of the string which satisfies the following condition:


Each character β\betaβ should appear at most f(β)f(\beta)f(β) times.
Here f(β)f(\beta)f(β) denotes the index of the character β\betaβ in the alphabet series. For example f(′a′)f('a')f(′a′) = 111, f(′b′)f('b')f(′b′) = 222 and so on.

**Note:** A substring of a string is a contiguous subsequence of that string.


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of every test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the string.
The second line of every test case contains a string SSS of size NNN.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line - the answer to the iii-th test case.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5*10^5
S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the string. The second line contains a string S of length N consisting only of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer – the maximum length of a contiguous substring of S in which every character β appears at most f(β) times, where f(β) is the position of β in the alphabet (a→1, b→2, …, z→26).

solution.approach: The condition for a substring is that for each character its frequency does not exceed its alphabet index. This is a classic sliding‑window (two‑pointers) problem. We maintain a window [left, right] and an array cnt[26] with the frequencies of characters inside the window. When we extend the window by moving right, we increase cnt of the new character. If its count becomes larger than its allowed limit (index+1), the window is shrunk from the left until the constraint is satisfied again. After each right move the current window length is a candidate for the answer. Because each pointer moves at most N steps, the algorithm runs in O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory (the 26‑size count array).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a fixed size array of 26 integers is used
